rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS ALAN GREENBERG VS. ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENT ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. All the authorities cited by the parties provide that when a party challenge as erroneous the methodology that an assessment appeals board uses, the petitioner was also seeking a refund of taxes paid. (Main & Von Karman Associates v. County of Orange (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 337, 339, 342.) Here, while the Petitioner challenges the methodology used by the Assessment Appeals Board of the City and County of San Francisco, the Petition does not seek a refund of real property taxes, and thus has not stated a claim upon which relief can be granted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY CHI HUA VS. DAMES SHOUMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT JESSICA ESMAY, JESSICA ESMAY AS TRUSTEE OF THE DELORISA TURNER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. The Court notes that both sides chose not to comply with procedural requirements for motions for summary judgment adjudication. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b) (Compare Notice of Motion and Separate Statement) and failed to tab its papers containing multiple exhibits; and Plaintiff failed to properly respond to the separate statement; failed to provide objections to evidence in the proper format; delivered a doble-sided courtesy copy. The Court will address the motion on the merits. The motion is denied as the moving party failed to shift their burden and there are multiple triable issues of facts. The facts included in Defendant's separate statement are not supported by evidence and/or are disputed: 10, 12, 16, 17, 19, 20 and 29. Furthermore, there is a dispute regarding the parties' rights, obligations and performance under paragraph 12 of the lease. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL FELDMAN, PH.D. VS. LINDA STEINHOFF HOLMES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Sfac 37.9(F)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT LINDA HOLMES NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO SFAC 37.9(F) is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that it is the "prevailing party" entitled to recovery of attorney's fees in this action that has not yet been reduced to a judgment or been dismissed. Defendant may file its motion for attorney's fees if necessary and proper upon entry of judgment or dismissal. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
